Did you lead the project?
Murielle, I love career stories. How did you start in HR?
I started fifteen years ago. My previous role was in a small consultancy.
Nice. What was your first big milestone?
I led a recruitment project for a new factory. We hired forty people in three months.
Impressive. Did you stay at that company for long?
No, I did not. After a merger, I moved to Minerva and got a promotion.
So the merger changed everything?
Yes, it did. It was hard, but I learned a lot and I never looked back.
I started my career fifteen years ago.
My first big milestone was a recruitment project.
The company went through a merger in 2018.
I got a promotion two years later.
Looking back, every step taught me something.

Hi Murielle, it is Liam again. You asked me about my career, so here is the short story. I started in HR almost fifteen years ago, in a small recruitment agency in Sydney. After three years, I moved to a big mining company, and that was a real milestone for me. I led my first project there: we launched a new onboarding program for the whole region. In 2018, the company went through a merger, and I learned a lot during that difficult time. I got a promotion two years later, and finally I joined the global people team. Looking back, every step taught me something. We should compare stories one day. Talk soon.

Good afternoon, everyone. I want to share a quick story from last year, because it was an important achievement for our department. When the restructuring started, many people felt nervous about the future. So we created a clear communication plan, and we held weekly meetings with every team. We did not promise easy answers, but we listened. In the end, we kept most of our key people, and the turnover stayed low. It was hard work, but it became one of my proudest career milestones. Thank you all for your trust during that time.
